defaultFrameType.label: Default Frame Type
defaultFrameType.help: Protocol type used when adding new frames (can be overridden per catalog)

maxRegisterErrors.label: Max Consecutive Register Errors
maxRegisterErrors.help: Stop polling a register group after this many consecutive read errors. Set to 0 to retry indefinitely.

preventIdleSleep.label: Prevent idle sleep during active sessions
preventIdleSleep.help: Keep the system awake while a session is actively streaming data

keepDisplayAwake.label: Keep display awake during active sessions
keepDisplayAwake.help: Prevent the display from turning off while a session is active

logLevel.label: Log Level
logLevel.help: Diagnostic log verbosity. Logs are written to ~/Documents/WireTAP/Reports/

smpPort.label: SMP UDP Port
smpPort.help: Default port for SMP firmware upgrade over UDP

language.title: Language
language.label: Display Language
language.help: Language used throughout the WireTAP interface. Additional locales will appear here as they become available.

appearance.label: Appearance
appearance.help: Choose light or dark theme, or follow your system preference.

frameIdFormat.label: Display Frame ID as
frameIdFormat.help: Choose how CAN frame IDs are shown in the editor.

saveFrameIdFormat.label: Save Frame ID Format
saveFrameIdFormat.help: Format used when saving frames to file

timeFormat.label: Display time as
timeFormat.help: Controls how timestamps are rendered in discovery and other views.

timezone.label: Default timezone
timezone.help: Default timezone for clock displays. Click the timezone badge in views to temporarily override.

signals.title: Signals
signals.help: Configure colours for signal confidence indicators.

binary.title: Binary Display
binary.help: Configure colours for binary bits in the Frame Calculator and bit previews.

frameEditor.title: Frame Editor
frameEditor.help: Colours for signal slots in the frame definition bit grid editor.

decoderDir.label: Decoder Directory
decoderDir.help: Directory where decoder catalog files (.toml) are stored

dumpDir.label: Dump Directory
dumpDir.help: Directory where CAN bus dump files are stored

reportDir.label: Report Directory
reportDir.help: Default directory for exported analysis reports

saveFrameIdFormat.label: Save frame ID format
saveFrameIdFormat.help: Controls default decoder file names when saving/creating catalogs.

storage.title: Storage
storage.label: Capture Storage
storage.help: Storage backend for captured frame data and imported captures.

clearOnStart.label: Clear captures on start
clearOnStart.help: Delete all captured frame and byte data when the app launches. Disable to preserve capture data across sessions (uses disk space).

discoveryHistory.label: Discovery History Buffer
discoveryHistory.help: Maximum number of frames to keep in memory during CAN Discovery

queryResultLimit.label: Query Result Limit
queryResultLimit.help: Maximum number of results returned by database queries in the Query app

graphBuffer.label: Graph Buffer Size
graphBuffer.help: Samples per signal in graph ring buffers. Higher values show more history but use more memory.

maxUnmatched.label: Max Unmatched Frames
maxUnmatched.help: Maximum frames kept in the unmatched (no catalog match) list

maxFiltered.label: Max Filtered Frames
maxFiltered.help: Maximum frames kept in the filtered (excluded by selection set) list

maxDecoded.label: Max Decoded Frames
maxDecoded.help: Maximum decoded frames displayed in the Decoder view

maxDecodedPerSource.label: Max Decoded Per Source
maxDecodedPerSource.help: Maximum decoded frames cached per unique source address. Higher values keep more history but use more memory.

maxHistory.label: Max History Items
maxHistory.help: Maximum number of transmitted frames to keep in the Transmit history log

telemetry.label: Send anonymous crash reports
telemetry.help: Help improve WireTAP by sending anonymous crash and error reports via Sentry. No personal data, CAN bus data, or usage analytics are collected — only error stack traces.

empty.heading: No catalogs found in decoder directory
No bookmarks saved yet
empty.description: Create bookmarks from the Decoder or Discovery apps

empty.heading: No selection sets saved yet
empty.description: Create selection sets from the Decoder or Discovery apps

empty.heading: No IO profiles configured
empty.description: Click "Add Profile" to create your first IO profile

common.saveFirstHint: Save the profile first to probe the device and configure interfaces.
common.clickProbeHint: Click "Probe Device" to detect available interfaces.

virtual.title: Virtual Device Settings
virtual.description: Generates synthetic traffic for testing without real hardware.

gvret.usbHint: Works with ESP32-RET, M2RET, CANDue, and other GVRET-compatible hardware over USB serial. Supports multi-bus devices and frame transmission.

framelink.title: FrameLink Connection
framelink.persistChanges: Save changes to device (persist across reboots)
framelink.configWillLoad: Configuration will be loaded when the device is reachable.

slcan.title: slcan Connection (CANable)
slcan.idleText: Select a port to check device
slcan.silentModeHint: Does not participate in bus arbitration. Ideal for passive monitoring.
slcan.fdHint: Enables CAN Flexible Data-rate for higher throughput and larger payloads (up to 64 bytes). Requires ELMUE CANable firmware with CAN FD support.
slcan.advancedHint: Most slcan devices use 8N1 (8 data bits, no parity, 1 stop bit). Only change these if your device requires different settings.
slcan.supportHint: Works with CANable, CANable Pro (slcan firmware), and other USB-CAN adapters using the Lawicel/slcan ASCII protocol.

socketcan.title: SocketCAN (Linux)
socketcan.fdHint: Enables CAN Flexible Data-rate. Requires FD-capable hardware.
socketcan.linuxHintRest: Works with CANable Pro (Candlelight firmware), native CAN hardware, or virtual CAN (vcan).
socketcan.configureAuto: WireTAP will configure the interface automatically (requires authentication).
socketcan.configureManual: Leave bitrate empty to use the interface as already configured by the system.

gsUsb.title: gs_usb (candleLight)
gsUsb.fdHint: Enables CAN Flexible Data-rate for higher throughput and larger payloads (up to 64 bytes).
gsUsb.supportHint: Works with CANable, CANable Pro (candleLight firmware), and other gs_usb-compatible devices.
gsUsb.macNote: macOS allows direct USB access - no driver needed.
gsUsb.linuxNote: On Linux, the kernel gs_usb driver exposes devices as SocketCAN interfaces.

empty.heading: No graph layouts saved yet
empty.description: Save layouts from the Graph app

confirmDelete.areYouSure: Are you sure you want to delete

ioDeviceStatus.selectPort: Select a port to check device

gsUsbDevicePicker.noDevicesFound: No gs_usb devices found. Connect a CANable or other candleLight device.
